data.title: Iranian Foreign Minister says he seeks a complete end to the war, not just a temporary ceasefire.
data.description: According to a Kyodo News report on the 21st, Iranian Foreign Minister Araqchi stated that Iran hopes for a complete, comprehensive, and lasting end to the war, not just a temporary ceasefire. In a telephone interview with a Kyodo News reporter on the 20th, Araqchi said that the attacks on Iran by the United States and Israel were illegal and unjustified acts of aggression, and he hoped the world would unite to oppose them. Several countries have begun to push for a ceasefire, but Iran is only considering a solution that ends the war decisively. Iran does not accept a temporary ceasefire but demands a complete end to the war, including ensuring that Iran will not be attacked again and compensation for the losses suffered. Araqchi also said he does not believe the United States is ready for negotiations. The report stated that Araqchi also indicated his intention to allow Japanese vessels to pass through the Strait of Hormuz, a vital energy transport route, after consultation with Japan. (Jinshi)

data.title: NATO Urged to Maintain Strong Deterrence Amid Middle East Focus
data.description: NATO must ensure it does not reduce its presence along its flanks to maintain a credible deterrence against Russian aggression, according to Norway's defense minister. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the importance of NATO's strategic positioning at a time when the U.S. is concentrating on the conflict in the Middle East. The minister emphasized that any retrenchment could weaken the alliance's ability to respond effectively to potential threats from Russia.

data.title: Ju.com's Meme-based IPO project, Punch, continues to hit new highs, with a more than 10-fold increase in just 3 days.
data.description: Ju.com's "Meme Phase 10 Initial Coin Offering Plan" Phase 2 project, PUNCH, has seen its price surge since launch, reaching a high of 4,700 USDT. This represents a more than 10-fold increase in the past 3 days, a 4.7 billion-fold increase from the initial offering price of 0.000001 USDT. For more details, please refer to Ju.com's official announcements and event page.

data.title: Thailand's Industrial GDP Faces Potential Decline Amid Middle East Tensions
data.description:
Thailand's Office of Industrial Economics (OIE) has issued a warning regarding the potential impact of rising crude oil prices on the country's industrial GDP. According to Jin10, if tensions in the Middle East lead to an increase in oil prices to $100-105 per barrel, Thailand's industrial GDP could shrink by 100-120 billion baht, accounting for approximately 0.15% of the sector's GDP.

The industries most likely to be affected are those with high energy consumption, including cement and concrete, glass and flat glass, ceramics and tiles, natural gas and petroleum, textiles and apparel, and pulp and paper. In contrast, Thailand's major high-value industries, such as electronics, gems and jewelry, hard disk drives, electrical appliances, and automotive manufacturing, have lower energy intensity and are expected to experience a more limited overall impact.

data.title: Shiba Inu ETF Speculation Linked to T. Rowe's Revised Filing
data.description: T. Rowe's recent revision of its ETF filing, associated with nearly $1.8 trillion in assets under management, is sparking bullish speculation around Shiba Inu. According to NS3.AI, the potential approval of a SHIB ETF could provide a liquidity boost for the cryptocurrency. However, the commentary also highlights that sustained demand would be crucial following any approval to maintain momentum.

data.title: Fire at South Korean Factory Claims 11 Lives, Search for Missing Continues
data.description:
A devastating fire at an auto parts manufacturing plant in Daejeon, central South Korea, has resulted in at least 11 fatalities and 59 injuries. According to RTHK, rescue teams discovered another body at noon today, while three individuals remain missing. The blaze erupted around 1 p.m. yesterday, with 170 workers present in the facility. Concerns over the building's structural integrity initially prevented firefighters from entering to conduct searches. The fire was eventually extinguished after approximately 10 and a half hours.

Rescue personnel are currently using heavy machinery to clear debris and continue the search for those still unaccounted for. Authorities have announced the formation of a special task force to handle the aftermath of the fire. A joint investigation with relevant departments will be conducted to determine the cause of the incident.

data.title: Iranian Media Reports Multiple Explosions in Eastern Tehran
data.description: At least three explosions have occurred in the eastern part of Tehran, Iran's capital. According to Jin10, the incidents were reported by Al Jazeera, highlighting the ongoing tensions in the region. Details about the cause and impact of the explosions remain unclear, and authorities have yet to release an official statement. The situation is developing, and further updates are expected as more information becomes available.

data.title: Iran's President Calls for Unity Among Islamic Nations
data.description: Iran's President Pezehiziyan has called for unity among Islamic countries and neighboring nations, emphasizing that they are considered brothers by Iran. According to Jin10, he stated that Iran does not wish to engage in any disputes with these nations. He further highlighted that Israel is the only entity that benefits from conflicts among them.

data.title: Bitcoin Mining Difficulty Drops 7.76% in Second-Largest Decrease of the Year
data.description: Bitcoin mining difficulty has decreased by 7.76%, bringing it down to 133.79 trillion. According to NS3.AI, this marks the second-largest decline in mining difficulty this year. The reduction in difficulty could impact miners' operations and profitability, as it adjusts the computational effort required to mine new blocks. This change reflects the ongoing fluctuations in the cryptocurrency mining landscape, influenced by various factors including market conditions and technological advancements.

data.title: Iranian Media Reports: Basij Militia Deputy Commander Azim Esmaili Killed
data.description: According to Jin10, Iranian media reports that Azim Esmaili, the deputy commander of the Basij militia, has been killed. The circumstances surrounding his death have not been detailed in the reports. The Basij militia is a paramilitary volunteer militia established in Iran, and it plays a significant role in the country's internal security and social order. Further information regarding the incident and its implications is awaited.

data.title: Kalshi's March Trading Volume Projected to Reach $12.6 Billion
data.description: Kalshi's trading volume for March is expected to hit approximately $12.6 billion if the current growth trend persists. According to NS3.AI, as of March 17, the platform's nominal monthly trading volume stood at around $6.9 billion, with an average daily volume of about $405 million.

data.title: Kalshi's $22 billion valuation surpasses that of many traditional sports betting giants.
data.description: Predictefy, a prediction market data platform, published an article on its X platform stating that prediction market platforms have surpassed traditional sports betting companies in valuation. Kalshi is currently valued at $22 billion, higher than all traditional sports betting companies. The valuation rankings of related entities are as follows: Kalshi $22 billion; FanDuel (Flutter) $19 billion; DraftKings $12.75 billion; bet365 $12 billion; BetMGM $9.4 billion; Polymarket $9 billion (valuation in its most recent funding round); CaesarsPalace $5.8 billion; betway (Super Group) $5.4 billion; EntainGroup $4.6 billion; ESPNBET $2.8 billion.

data.title: Hong Kong police revealed that a retiree was defrauded of HK$6.6 million over six months after being misled three times by a "cryptocurrency expert."
data.description: Hong Kong Police Cyber ​​Crime Bureau's "Cyber ​​Guardians" have disclosed a cryptocurrency scam case. A 66-year-old retiree was repeatedly deceived by a "cryptocurrency investment expert" since September 2025. After being defrauded of HK$1.4 million in the first instance, the victim paid another HK$600,000 deposit to another "expert" in an attempt to recover his losses. He was then induced to purchase HK$4.6 million worth of cryptocurrency, which was transferred to a designated account. The scammers disappeared after receiving the money. Within six months, the victim's total losses reached HK$6.6 million before he finally reported the incident to the police. The police remind the public not to easily believe in "guaranteed profit" investments and so-called "loss recovery" services to avoid being scammed in a series of scams. (Hong Kong Wen Wei Po)

data.title: Data: Kalshi's notional trading volume in March is expected to reach $12.6 billion.
data.description: KalshiData stated on its X platform that as of March 17, Kalshi's nominal monthly transaction volume was approximately $6.9 billion, with an average daily transaction volume of approximately $405 million. If this growth rate continues, Kalshi's nominal transaction volume in March is projected to reach $12.6 billion.

data.title: Iran Threatens Global Retaliation Amid Ongoing Tensions with Israel
data.description: Iran has issued a warning to expand its retaliatory measures to global tourist destinations, raising concerns about potential attacks beyond the Middle East. According to Ming Pao, this development follows three weeks of Israeli airstrikes on Iran, with no signs of de-escalation. The threat from Tehran is seen as a strategy to pressure the United States amid the ongoing conflict.

data.title: German Conservative Party Faces Another Regional Election Challenge
data.description: Friedrich Merz is set to attempt another regional election victory on Sunday following a significant loss for the German chancellor's conservative party in a prosperous southwestern state two weeks ago. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the upcoming electoral challenge for Merz and his party. The recent defeat has put pressure on the conservatives to regain their footing in the political landscape. As the election approaches, Merz aims to rally support and secure a win to bolster the party's standing. The outcome of this election could have implications for the party's future strategy and influence in regional politics.

data.title: Iran's Revolutionary Guards: UAE's Ras Al Khaimah Warned of Potential Attack
data.description: On March 21, according to Iranian media, Iran has issued a warning to residents of Ras Al Khaimah in the United Arab Emirates, urging them to evacuate via designated routes. According to Jin10, the warning comes as Ras Al Khaimah is reportedly being used for operations against Iranian islands, making it a potential target for future attacks. Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guards Corps (IRGC) stated on social media that if Iranian islands are attacked again from the UAE, Ras Al Khaimah will face significant consequences.

data.title: Iran Launches Ballistic Missile with Cluster Bomb Warhead at Israel, Israeli Military Reports
data.description: On March 21, according to Jin10, the Israeli Defense Forces issued a statement reporting that Iran launched a ballistic missile equipped with a cluster bomb warhead towards central Israel. The missile caused damage at three locations in Rishon LeZion, as confirmed by Israeli rescue teams. The incident marks a significant escalation in tensions between the two nations.

data.title: Iran's Internet Shutdown Obscures Civilian Presence in Modern Conflict
data.description: Iran's recent internet shutdown has resulted in a unique situation where civilian presence in the ongoing conflict is largely absent from online platforms. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting how this digital blackout has made it difficult for the global community to access real-time information and visuals from the ground. This move by the Iranian government has effectively limited the flow of information, creating a modern conflict scenario where the usual smartphone footage and civilian accounts are missing. The shutdown has raised concerns about transparency and the ability to monitor the situation accurately.

data.title: 4.0 Magnitude Earthquake Strikes Iran's Hamadan Province
data.description: A 4.0 magnitude earthquake occurred in Iran's Hamadan Province at 3:23 a.m. local time on March 21, according to Jin10. The crisis management department of Hamadan Province reported that the earthquake's epicenter was located approximately 6 kilometers north of Firuzan, near the border between Hamadan and Kermanshah provinces. The quake's depth was recorded at about 5 kilometers.
